title: E.coil perturbation intake
description: Structured output the LLM emits when grounding a free-text / uploaded perturbation request into canonical graph entry points. Used as vLLM guided_json so the model cannot invent ids outside the supplied candidates.

entity_id.description: Canonical graph id of the ENTRY POINT to perturb. For an exogenous agent (a drug/chemical), this is the endogenous TARGET inside the cell, not the drug itself.

target_state.description: The state the entry point takes after the perturbation; must be one of that entity's allowed_states.

agent_mention.description: The span of user text this came from (e.g. 'ciprofloxacin', 'knock out recA').

evidence.description: Which candidate annotation/edge justifies this entry point. Grounds the choice so a human can audit it.

unresolved.description: Mentions the model could not confidently ground. Surfaced for human judgment rather than guessed.
